clusters.root_cause_summary: Stair centroid lies in a navmesh-disconnected island (Reach_stair_centroid persistently False across 5+ distinct centroids in the same ~0.9m region). All nine candidates targeted look_for_downstair with exit conditions, but look_for_downstair exits naturally after 2–12 steps in both scenes — the actual prolonged stall is in get_close_to_stair (75 steps in qyAac8rV8Zk; onset ~step 179 in q3zU7Yy5E5s), a fundamentally different FSM mode that no tested mechanism has addressed with an exit condition.
clusters.cross_scene_pattern: Identical failure skeleton: explore → look_for_downstair (exits naturally, ≤12 steps) → look_up_back → explore → get_close_to_stair (stall). Both scenes share: five-or-more distinct centroid samples across the same disconnected ~0.9m stair region; identical behavioral fingerprints across all candidates; and pathfinder.find_path() returning false-feasible results that allow get_close_to_stair re-entry despite navmesh disconnection, confirmed by 27–35 consecutive Reach_stair_centroid=False messages. Key differences: stall onset step differs (q3zU7Yy5E5s ~179 vs qyAac8rV8Zk ~164), stall duration differs (q3zU7Yy5E5s unbounded vs qyAac8rV8Zk 75 steps), and look_for_downstair natural exit length differs (12 vs 2 steps) — same disconnection mechanism, scene-specific FSM timing.
clusters.transfer_notes: Candidates 6/7/8 applied three successive exit conditions to look_for_downstair (no-progress counter N=15, step budget T=40, pre-entry pathfinder gate) and produced zero behavioral change in both scenes — this eliminates look_for_downstair as a causal node and points unambiguously at get_close_to_stair as the blocking mode. Candidate_5's 45-step extension of look_for_downstair in q3zU7Yy5E5s (steps 70–115) confirmed the FSM transition path: extended look_for_downstair → explore → get_close_to_stair, establishing that the stall re-enters via get_close_to_stair regardless of what happens in look_for_downstair. The pathfinder.find_path() false-feasibility mechanism (snapping to nearby navigable nodes) is the reason pre-entry gating failed; navmesh island_radius queries are needed to supplement find_path() for reliable disconnection detection.
clusters.highest_leverage_untested: step_budget_or_reach_centroid_false_count_exit_on_get_close_to_stair: add an exit condition directly to the get_close_to_stair FSM mode (e.g., exit after N consecutive Reach_stair_centroid=False or after T steps without min_dis_to_downstair decrease), combined with a navmesh island_radius check at stair commitment time to verify the centroid shares a connected component with the agent — if infeasible, permanently disable the stair and regenerate frontiers with a wider search radius

clusters.root_cause_summary: candidate_0 already solves this scene via passive climb_stair (steps 91–102, floor_step resets at step 287, DTG ~0.04). The hysteresis patch in candidate_2 disrupts this proven path by forcing get_close_to_stair→look_for_downstair oscillation from step 118, inflating floor_reinits from 3 to 6 and exhausting 499 steps. The toilet is confirmed 2D-feasible (failure tag 'feasible'), making this a pure patch-induced regression on an otherwise solvable episode.
clusters.cross_scene_pattern: Single-scene cluster. The defining structural feature is that the target IS reachable and WAS reached in candidate_0 — this makes mL8ThkuaVTM categorically different from all other scenes where no candidate ever accessed the goal floor successfully. The hysteresis patch triggered a stair approach mode change during explore, converting a passive non-stair-mode climb into an active oscillating stair mode cycle.
clusters.transfer_notes: This scene is a transfer warning for the get_close_to_stair_navmesh_stall cluster: candidate_0's success came precisely because it did NOT invoke get_close_to_stair or look_for_downstair actively — passive climb_stair detection during explore is a distinct FSM path. Any patch that adds exit conditions to get_close_to_stair must guard against suppressing passive stair detection during explore, otherwise scenes like mL8ThkuaVTM will regress. The oscillation pattern (get_close_to_stair→look_for_downstair→get_close_to_stair) in candidate_2 also confirms that hysteresis gating alone cannot prevent re-entry loops if the underlying stair mode transition logic is not preserved.
clusters.highest_leverage_untested: revert_hysteresis_patch_and_preserve_passive_climb_stair_detection_during_explore: remove or conditionalize the hysteresis patch so the passive climb_stair code path during explore mode is not intercepted; candidate_0 already proves this yields SUCCESS with high confidence

clusters.root_cause_summary: Stair is successfully traversed (climb_stair success at step 198, floor_step resets to 0), but floor 2 exposes only 2 frontiers near the stair landing (max semantic score 0.532 at 0.9m), exhausted in ~16 floor_steps. The bed room lies in a navmesh-disconnected component of floor 2 inaccessible from the stair landing; T4_NOQUIT rescues at steps 228 and 241 both found an empty frontier pool, confirming the failure is structural disconnection, not a time or budget issue.
clusters.cross_scene_pattern: Single-scene cluster. Unique among all scenes: stair traversal succeeds (this is the only cluster where the agent reaches the target floor). The failure mode is purely post-climb floor-2 exploration coverage — the accessible navmesh island on floor 2 is a small stair-landing stub that does not connect to the room containing the goal. All 8 candidates have identical behavioral fingerprints because no intervention reaches the post-climb causal path (all tested levers target stair approach or traversal modes).
clusters.transfer_notes: T4_NOQUIT's empty frontier pool at steps 228/241 is the key diagnostic: it proves that neither extended budget nor frontier regeneration under current radius settings can help, because the navigable floor-2 island is physically too small. candidate_8's pre-entry pathfinder gate was structurally unreachable for this scene (look_for_downstair never stalls here), confirming the causal structure is entirely orthogonal to the get_close_to_stair_navmesh_stall cluster. The fix must operate post-climb: either detect small accessible island area before committing to floor-2 exploration, or use 3D raycasting/room-boundary detection to seed frontiers beyond the stair landing.
clusters.highest_leverage_untested: navmesh_island_radius_check_post_climb_with_frontier_seeding_beyond_stair_landing: after stair traversal, query island_radius at the stair landing point on floor 2; if the accessible island is below a minimum area threshold (indicating a stair-landing stub), trigger frontier seeding via 3D raycast or room-boundary expansion to find navigable nodes beyond the stub, or fall back to searching for an alternate floor-2 entry point

Tested in candidate_7 on both scenes; no-op because look_for_downstair natural exit (2–12 steps) is far below any reasonable budget threshold T
Tested in candidate_6 on both scenes; no-op because look_for_downstair natural exit (2–12 steps) fires before the N=15 counter accumulates

Tested in both scenes; stair re-enables via a separate code path ('Navigating upstairs to unexplored floor'), confirming permanent-disable logic is bypassed by the get_close_to_stair re-entry mechanism — the stall mode is unaffected

Ruled out for mL8ThkuaVTM: failure is a patch-induced disruption of a passive stair path on a 2D-feasible episode, not a floor-switching frequency problem; increasing DP12 does not restore the passive climb_stair code path
Tested in XB4GS9ShBRE: rescues at steps 228 and 241 both found empty frontier pool, confirming the failure is a structural navmesh disconnection and additional episode budget is orthogonal to the fix
